segments.text:
Recombinant DNA Techniques
isolation and characterization of specific genes
vectors
virus/bacteriophage – l, adenovirus
plasmid
(artificial chromosome)
enzymes
restriction endonuclease
DNA phosphatase/T4 kinase
ligase
Klenow DNA pol I
reverse transcriptase
construction, screening of
gene libraries
ways to introduce DNA
into cells

Library = collection of DNA fragments
one per vector (usually phage)
a l cloning vector for library construction

A more recent, more heavily engineered l vector
insert size range = 2 - 20 kb

Genomic library construction
need genomic DNA fragments 10-20 kb
don’t cleave every DNA
molecule at every site

frequency of recognition site = once every 46 bp = every 4096 bp
most restriction enzymes cut at 4-base, 6-base or 8-base sites

Genomic library construction
cut genomic DNA and l vector
with same restriction enzyme(s)
mix DNAs and ligate
add phage proteins
“packaging mix”

A phage library – each phage has a piece of genomic DNA inserted into it
Every fragment of genome is present
Proportional representation – abundance in library reflects abundance in genome

cDNA library construction
cDNA = complementary DNA
DNA copies of mRNAs from source cells/tissue
will ONLY contain clones from regions of DNA being transcribed
abundance of cDNA reflects abundance of mRNA in source cells
choose cells/tissue, conditions to maximize expression
mRNA = 1-4% of all RNA in cells
isolate RNA, then select for mRNAs

cDNA synthesis
first strand synthesis – produces DNA-RNA hybrid
add oligo-dT primer
extend primer with reverse transcriptase

cDNA synthesis – second strand
remove RNA with RNase H
add random primers
extend with DNA pol
NNN
NNN
NNN
5’
AAAAA3’
double-stranded DNA
blunt-end clone or add adaptors
ligate into l vector
package into recombinant phage

add terminal transferase and dCTP
cDNA synthesis – second strand, v2
remove RNA with RNase H
add oligo-dG primers
extend with DNA pol
5’GGGG
3’
double-stranded DNA

Library screening
hybridization screening
works for any DNA
requires that you know something
about the DNA sequence you’re
looking for
expression screening
only useful for cDNA libraries
requires directional cloning,
expression vector
generally requires antibody
against protein of interest

Library screening – the general idea
infect E. coli cells with phage from library
immobilize infected cells in nutrient agar before lysis
progeny phage only infect adjacent cells
plaque = cleared zone, cells lysed
all phage there = progeny of original infecting phage

Hybridization screening
probe can be:
heterologous gene
oligonucleotide
partial clone
Note: DNA is double-stranded
both strands present on filter
orientation of clone in vector
doesn’t matter

Oligonucleotide probe – based on known or predicted amino acid sequence
Amino acids from 		Met  Gly  Cys  Ala  Phe  Arg
N-terminal amino
acid sequencing
Possible DNA			ATG 	GGA	TGC GCA TTC AGA
sequences				     C	     T      C	    T C   C
G		    G		    G
T		    T		    T
Degeneracy of				     4	 x  2  x  4  x  2x2x4 = 512-fold
probe
“Probe” would be a mix of 512 different oligonucleotides
Screening using partial clone as probe – chromosome “walking”

Approach commonly used to clone disease genes
(cystic fibrosis gene)

Expression screening
requires “expression vector” = vector with inducible promoter adjacent to
DNA cloning/insertion site
requires cDNA cloned in correct orientation - directional cloning
requires way to identify clone expressing protein of interest
usually tagged antibody/antiserum that recognizes protein
A simple expression vector
induce expression of inserted cDNA in phage

Modified expression screen: Yeast Two-hybrid Screen
based on protein-protein interaction
can find unknown interaction partners
doesn’t require you know anything about cDNA you’re looking for
Uses expression plasmid vectors
one = “bait”, same in all cells
other = “prey”, from expression library
different clone from library in each cell/colony
fusion protein:
DNA binding domain of known transcription factor
fused in frame with “bait” = cDNA of protein you
know about

fusion protein from clone in library
transactivation domain of known
transcription factor
fused in frame with cloned cDNA
different in every cell
can “go fishing” in library for EVERY cDNA protein product that binds
to your bait construct
interaction between
two fusion proteins
generates functional
transcription factor
Yeast Two-hybrid Screen

Yeast Two-hybrid Screen
look for colonies
expressing reporter

reporter often = lacZ, screen for blue colonies
sometimes gene for production of required nutrient
